_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 7 currently banned, 6322 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: fail2ban total bans 6322 (up from 6221, +101). 7 IPs currently banned (up from 5). 5 currently failed. Brute-force continues at typical rate. No successful logins — only automated ssh-rsa rejections and authorized sudo from novakj.

pdf_security.document_write_safe: 1 usage in keyboard-shortcuts.js line 395 — writes to newly opened blank window for print cheat sheet only. All content escaped via escapeHtml(). Acceptable pattern.
pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 178 usages across 44 files (up from 174/44 — snip.js added with 5 usages: 2 clearing containers, 1 static HTML, 1 static panel template, 1 history rendering with internally-generated values only).

snip_js.feature: Snip tool — draw rectangular selections on PDF pages and extract regions as PNG, JPG, cropped PDF, or text. Supports mouse/touch input, selection resizing/moving, keyboard shortcuts, clipboard copy, and history.
snip_js.escape_function: No escapeHtml defined — not needed since no user-controlled strings are inserted via innerHTML. History panel uses only internally-generated values (pageNum integer, hardcoded format strings, Date.toLocaleTimeString()).
snip_js.innerHTML_usage: 5 usages — line 400: svg.innerHTML='' (clear), line 854: panel.innerHTML='' (clear), line 857: static empty-state message, line 865: history items with internally-generated values only (pageNum, format, timestamp), line 951: static panel template with no dynamic data. All safe.
snip_js.text_display: Uses textContent throughout for dynamic text: SVG badge text (line 493), dimension labels (line 518), button labels (line 597), count badges (line 834), slider values (line 1008). Correct pattern.
snip_js.pdf_processing: Operates on already-loaded state.pdfDocument — does not handle file loading or validation. File validation is upstream in app.js via validatePdf() from utils.js.
snip_js.download_filenames: Constructed from state.currentFile.name with '-snip' suffix, passed through downloadBlob() which calls sanitizeFilename(). Safe.
snip_js.canvas_rendering: Resolution multiplier up to 4x. Large pages at 4x could use significant memory but bounded by single-page processing. Low risk.
snip_js.selections_array: Unbounded — no maximum selection count enforced. Low risk since user-driven.
snip_js.history_array: Properly bounded at 10 entries via pop() after unshift(). Good practice.
snip_js.data_privacy: No server calls, no external requests, no analytics. All processing in browser memory.

app_js_update.change: Added 'snip' to viewer container visibility list (line 62) — shows PDF viewer when snip tool is active. Minimal change, no security impact.

known_issues: server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this.
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot.
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed.
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.

merge.js, batch.js, hyperlinks.js, bates.js, command-palette.js, keyboard-shortcuts.js, form-data.js, link-manager.js use escapeHtml()/escHtml() that does not escape double quotes. CSP script-src 'self' mitigates inline handler injection. In most files used in element content (safe). In attribute contexts (title/aria-label/value/data-col) should add escapeAttr().

hyperlinks.js: detected PDF link URLs not validated for protocol scheme. A malicious PDF could contain javascript: URIs in link annotations. CSP + noopener/noreferrer mitigate. Should add protocol allowlist (http/https/mailto) before window.open().
hyperlinks.js line 686: email link window.open() missing noopener/noreferrer — minor since mailto: links are low risk.
img2pdf.js image validation uses OR logic (MIME type OR extension) - slightly permissive but acceptable for client-side processing.
insert-pages.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
metadata.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
batch.js line 294: escapeHtml() used with textContent causes double-escaping — cosmetic display bug for filenames containing &, <, >.
keyboard-shortcuts.js line 395: document.write used on new popup window for print cheat sheet — acceptable pattern but noted for tracking.
headers-footers.js: custom preset names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, only affects local storage size.
form-data.js: template names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, same pattern as headers-footers.js.

Session restore (TASK-036): restoreNotes/restoreAnnotations/restoreDrawings/restoreSignatures/restoreFormValues do not validate data schema from IndexedDB. IndexedDB is same-origin so risk is self-XSS only. Low priority.
link-manager.js Find & Replace: new RegExp() from user input could cause ReDoS with pathological patterns. Low impact — only runs against URL strings (typically short). Errors caught.
compare.js escapeHtml() does not escape double quotes — same pattern as other modules. Only used in element content context in HTML report, not in attributes. Safe.

snip.js line 865: innerHTML with template literal uses internally-generated values (pageNum, format, timestamp) — not user-controlled. LOW risk but should ideally use textContent/DOM API for defense-in-depth.
snip.js: no escapeHtml utility defined — acceptable since no user-controlled strings are inserted via innerHTML, but would be good practice to add.
snip.js: selections array unbounded — no max limit. Low risk since user-driven interaction.
snip.js line 661: raw err.message from clipboard API shown in toast — minor info disclosure, clipboard errors are browser-generated.
notes: Security review completed — 100 checks passed, 0 fixes needed this run.

REVIEWED: snip.js (1075 lines). Snip/region extraction tool: draw rectangles on PDF pages, extract as PNG/JPG/PDF/text, clipboard copy, selection history. No eval, no Function, no document.write, no window.open, no fetch. Uses textContent throughout for dynamic text. innerHTML usages all safe (clearing containers, static HTML, or internally-generated values only). Downloads use sanitizeFilename via downloadBlob. Fully client-side. SECURE.
REVIEWED: app.js update — added 'snip' to viewer container visibility list (line 62). Minimal change, no security impact.
JS file count: 58 (up from 57 — snip.js added).
innerHTML count: 178 across 44 files (up from 174/44 — snip.js adds 5 usages, but compare.js likely lost 1 in rewrite, net +4).

SSH: fail2ban total bans 6322 (+101 since last review). 7 IPs currently banned (up from 5). 5 currently failed. No successful logins — only brute-force attempts on root and invalid users. Only authorized cron/sudo from novakj.
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (75 days remaining).
All security headers confirmed in HTTP response. Nginx blocking rules verified (git/env/CLAUDE.md/sh/traversal all return 404).
recommendations: MEDIUM-LOW: Add protocol allowlist to hyperlinks.js handleLinkClick() — only allow http:, https:, mailto: schemes before calling window.open() on detected PDF links

LOW: Add escapeAttr() function to merge.js, batch.js, hyperlinks.js, bates.js, command-palette.js, keyboard-shortcuts.js, form-data.js, link-manager.js for attribute contexts instead of escapeHtml()/escHtml()
LOW: Fix batch.js line 294 — use fileName directly with textContent instead of escapeHtml(fileName)
LOW: Set server_tokens off in nginx.conf to stop leaking version info (currently exposes nginx/1.26.3)
LOW: Enable UFW firewall (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
LOW: Add general *.md deny rule to nginx with explicit exception for known safe files
LOW: Remove dead escapeHtml() function from insert-pages.js and metadata.js
LOW: Remove unused CGI location block from nginx config
LOW: Consider disable_symlinks on in nginx for defense in depth
LOW: Add length limit to custom preset names in headers-footers.js saveCustomPreset() and form-data.js saveTemplate()
LOW: Add basic type/schema validation in session restore functions for defense in depth
LOW: snip.js line 865 — replace innerHTML template literal with textContent/DOM API for defense-in-depth
